provenance.upstream_generation_method: curated starter baseline with declared framings

evidence.title: Individual lipid responses to dietary cholesterol from eggs vary
evidence.claim: Individuals differ in their serum-lipid response to dietary cholesterol from eggs, so a population-average association does not fully answer whether a particular person will experience a clinically relevant change.
evidence.evidence_quality: Controlled feeding studies support the existence of heterogeneous biomarker responses, but many are small, short, and use egg doses above ordinary consumption. Definitions of a “hyper-responder” vary, and long-term clinical relevance remains less certain than the short-term lipid response.
evidence.strongest_counterargument: The heterogeneity may have limited clinical importance if LDL and HDL change together, if effects remain within healthy ranges, or if the observed response does not translate into different cardiovascular outcomes. Small studies can also exaggerate apparent responder subgroups.
evidence.confidence_assessment: No numeric confidence is assigned. The evidence supports splitting a single population-level claim into a population warrant and a person-specific uncertainty statement; it does not support diagnosing an individual from the literature alone.

citations.citation: Katan, M. B., Beynen, A. C., de Vries, J. H. M., & Nobels, A. (1986). Existence of consistent hypo- and hyperresponders to dietary cholesterol in man. *American Journal of Epidemiology* 123(2):221–234. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/3946374/

Beynen, A. C., Katan, M. B., & van Zutphen, L. F. M. (1987). Hypo- and hyperresponders: individual differences in the response of serum cholesterol concentration to changes in diet. *Advances in Lipid Research* 22:115–171. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/3328489/

Godos, J., et al. (2021). Egg consumption and cardiovascular risk: a dose-response meta-analysis of prospective cohort studies. *European Journal of Nutrition* 60:1833–1862. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00394-020-02345-7

unattributed: The baseline assessment has not yet been independently audited.

notes: Baseline prose and scores are preserved as claims by the upstream run, not adopted as our judgments.
